evals.prompt:
I want to build a personal portfolio site. Here's my background — please use the dark-tech theme.

Name: Maya Chen
Location: San Francisco, CA
Current role: Staff ML Engineer at Vega AI (Aug 2022 – present)
- Built and shipped a real-time feature platform serving 12B daily inferences across recommendations, search, and ads. Cut p99 latency from 280ms to 65ms.
- Led a team of 6 engineers redesigning the model training pipeline; reduced training cost by 38% ($1.4M/year) by switching to spot GPUs and gradient checkpointing.
- Owns the on-call rotation for the inference layer; reduced sev-1 incidents from 8/quarter to 1/quarter through better observability.
- Tech: Python, PyTorch, Ray, Kubernetes, Triton, Postgres, Kafka.

Prior: Senior ML Engineer at Stripe (2019–2022)
- Built fraud detection models that prevented an estimated $40M in losses annually
- Led migration from XGBoost to deep tabular models

ML Engineer at Lyft (2017–2019)
- Worked on ETA prediction models

Education: M.S. Computer Science, Stanford 2017. B.S. EECS, UC Berkeley 2015.

GitHub: github.com/mayachen-ml (12 repos, ~800 stars on a vector-search library)
LinkedIn: linkedin.com/in/mayachen-ml
Email: maya@mayachen.dev

Projects:
- pyvecindex (open source) — fast vector index for Python, 800 stars on GitHub
- mlflow-helm-chart — Production-ready Helm chart for MLflow on Kubernetes

Value prop I want: 'I build ML systems that ship — and keep shipping at scale.'
evals.expected_output: A portfolio HTML in dark-tech theme + a markdown resume. Should use Maya's exact value prop. Should include all 3 jobs, both projects, GitHub link, LinkedIn link, email contact. Should run a gap analysis surfacing the vague Lyft bullet ('worked on ETA prediction').
expectations: An HTML file is produced that uses the dark-tech theme (dark background, monospace font references in CSS)
A markdown resume file is produced
Maya Chen's name appears in both files
All three job titles (Staff ML Engineer at Vega AI, Senior ML Engineer at Stripe, ML Engineer at Lyft) appear in both files
The Stripe '$40M in losses annually' achievement appears in both files
The pyvecindex project appears in the portfolio
The vague Lyft bullet 'worked on ETA prediction' is flagged in a gap analysis section in the chat response (not silently rewritten)
The user's exact value prop appears in the portfolio header
GitHub link to mayachen-ml is present
No fabricated metrics appear that weren't in Maya's input

evals.prompt:
Make me a portfolio. I'm a designer. Use the creative-bold theme.

Name: Jordan Park
Location: Brooklyn, NY
Title: Senior Product Designer

Experience:
- Senior Product Designer at Notion, 2023–present. Led design for Notion AI's writing surface. Shipped 4 major releases. Bumped activation rate of new AI users from 12% to 31%.
- Product Designer at Figma, 2020–2023. Owned design for FigJam from 0 → 1. Helped grow MAU from 0 to 2.5M in two years.
- Product Designer at Square, 2018–2020. Worked on the seller dashboard.

Education: BFA, RISD 2018.

Links: jordanpark.design, twitter.com/jordandesigns (18k followers), behance.net/jordanpark

No GitHub. Email: hi@jordanpark.design.
evals.expected_output: A portfolio HTML in creative-bold theme + a markdown resume. Should not include any GitHub references (user has none). Should include Behance and Twitter links. Gap analysis should flag missing project case studies (designers need them) and the vague Square bullet.
expectations: An HTML file is produced that uses the creative-bold theme (vibrant colors, large display font)
A markdown resume file is produced
Jordan Park's name appears in both files
All three roles (Notion, Figma, Square) appear in both files
The 12% to 31% activation metric appears in both files
The Twitter/X link is present
The Behance link is present
GitHub is NOT present anywhere (user has no GitHub)
Gap analysis flags the vague Square bullet ('worked on the seller dashboard')
Gap analysis flags missing project case studies as something a designer needs

evals.prompt:
Hey can you put together a portfolio + resume from this stuff? Just pick whatever theme makes sense.

I'm Sam Rivera, a junior developer in Austin. Graduated from UT Austin in 2024 (CS). I've been doing freelance frontend work for about 8 months now.

Projects:
- Built a small SaaS tool that helps teachers generate quiz questions from lesson plans (uses GPT-4). Has 40 paying customers. quizforge.app
- Open source: a CLI tool for resizing images that I made for myself, ended up with a few hundred users. github.com/samrivera/imgcli
- A redesign of my college's CS department website (volunteer)

Skills: JavaScript, TypeScript, React, Next.js, Tailwind, Node, some Python.

GitHub: github.com/samrivera. Email: sam@samrivera.dev.

No LinkedIn yet (need to make one).
evals.expected_output: A portfolio + resume. Theme is the skill's choice (likely minimal-light or dark-tech given dev background). Should include all 3 projects with quizforge having paying customers highlighted. Gap analysis should flag missing LinkedIn as critical for job search.
expectations: An HTML file is produced
A markdown resume file is produced
Sam Rivera's name appears in both files
The skill picks a theme and explains the choice (minimal-light or dark-tech, not creative-bold)
All three projects (quizforge.app, imgcli, CS dept redesign) appear in the portfolio
The '40 paying customers' detail appears for quizforge
The GitHub link to samrivera appears
Gap analysis flags missing LinkedIn as critical or moderate severity
Gap analysis suggests adding metrics to the imgcli project ('a few hundred users' is vague)
